data.body:
[G3 child-coverage — container review of parent 1f19-adf2-ffb2-4b09 paired with THIS child (S1 6d56: Faithful, lock-free snapshot materialization core)]

Audited this child's contribution to the parent's acceptance/success criteria; every absence cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (S2 70c7 cache+janitor, S3 fa49 gate threading, S4 30b3 attested signing, S5 b7fa CLI/MCP surfacing, S6 1ba5 docs). This child is the materialization-core slice; the union of children covers every parent criterion. No uncovered parent criterion. EMPTY G3 findings list.

4-bucket coverage map (parent AC -> covering child/children; this child's role called out):
- Parent AC1 (no attested gate reads the working tree; all read a faithful snapshot materialized at the pinned SHA) — FULLY covered, SPLIT: THIS child is the PRIMARY OWNER of the faithful-materialization half — its AC1 (materialize(ref) produces a directory byte-matching the committed tree at the resolved SHA, INCLUDING export-ignore files; faithful, not git archive) + its AC2 (LFS pointer detection; submodule handling defined/documented). The re-rooting half (point the gate's file tools at the snapshot, never the working tree) is S3 (fa49 AC1/AC4/AC5). Heart of this child. Clean.
- Parent AC2 (signed verdict records verified_at_sha + reproducible over identical content) — FULLY covered by S4 (30b3 record+sign+in-toto) atop the immutable-SHA basis THIS child supplies (its What: "resolve ref ... to an immutable SHA against origin" + AC1 byte-faithful materialization is what makes "a verdict over identical content" achievable). STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child for the signing act itself (its Scope: "Out: ... signing (S4)"). Not an absence (S4 covers signing).
- Parent AC3 (local mode reads in-place checkout AND cannot produce a signed verdict) — covered by S3 (in-place read) + S4 (signing bar). This child notes only the materialize-side fail behavior ("attested mode fails closed, local mode still runs"); it does not own the in-place read or the sign-bar (its Scope: "Out: ... gate wiring (S3); signing (S4)"). STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child. Not an absence.
- Parent AC4 (concurrent gates on same/different SHAs run without git-lock contention or cross-op corruption; no worktrees; atomic population; delete-on-last-close) — FULLY covered, SPLIT cleanly: THIS child OWNS the atomic-population + no-worktree + no index.lock/config.lock-contention half — its AC3 (population is atomic: a reader never observes a partial tree; a crashed materialization leaves only a tmp/ dir, cleaned on startup), its AC6 (concurrent materialize() of different SHAs do not contend on index.lock/config.lock), and the no-worktree read-tree+checkout-index design in its What. S2 (70c7) owns the single-flight + delete-on-last-close half (its AC1/AC2). No double-implementation; no gap.
- Parent AC5 (snapshot cache + janitor reclaim disk under pressure without disrupting in-flight reads) — FULLY covered by S2 (70c7, sole owner). Explicitly out of this child's scope (its Scope: "Out: the cache/janitor (S2)"). STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child. Not an absence.
- Parent AC6 (private-repo fetch failure surfaces a descriptive, actionable credential error) — FULLY covered, with THIS child as the RAISE-side PRIMARY OWNER: its AC5 (a private-repo fetch with missing credentials raises a descriptive, actionable error; attested mode fails closed) — the coalesced git fetch origin is this child's only lock-taking step, so the error originates here. S6 (1ba5 AC1/AC2) documents the requirement + message + remedy + fail-closed/local-still-runs. Raise + document pairing complete. Clean.
- Parent AC7 (portable: no external tar; no hardcoded /tmp; flock+mkdir fallback) — FULLY covered, with THIS child as the IMPLEMENTATION PRIMARY OWNER: its AC4 (no external tar binary invoked; no hardcoded /tmp; runs on macOS + Linux) + its What portability bullet (extraction never shells to external tar; temp root from tempfile.gettempdir()/mkdtemp 0700, overridable via REBAR_GATE_TMPDIR, created OUTSIDE the repo/.git; fcntl.flock with atomic-mkdir fallback on a local FS). S6 (1ba5 AC5) documents REBAR_GATE_TMPDIR + the EFS/NFS flock caveat. Implementation + docs split clean. Clean.
- Parent AC8 (all six child stories closed; make lint + full test suite green) — STRUCTURAL/meta epic-completion criterion; covered by the existence of all six children (this child is story #1 of 6 in the roster) + each child's own green-bar obligation. Not an absence at this child.

Success-criteria narrative ("every code-reading gate verifies a client-specified, immutable, reproducible snapshot of the project at a pinned SHA — never the server's mutable checkout — safely under concurrent use ...; signed verdicts reproducible and bound to verified_at_sha; the unsigned local mode preserves verify-before-push") decomposes onto AC1-AC7. The "immutable, reproducible snapshot ... at a pinned SHA" clause is precisely this child's core deliverable: resolve->pin->FAITHFULLY materialize (byte-match including export-ignore, LFS-aware, submodule-defined) is what makes the snapshot reproducible content rather than the lossy git-archive output; the immutable-SHA resolution it owns is the basis S4's signed reproducibility (AC2) and S2's content-addressed cache key (no staleness) both rest on. The "safely under concurrent use" clause is co-owned: this child's atomic population + no-index.lock-contention (AC3/AC6) plus S2's single-flight + delete-on-last-close. The "client-specified"/surfacing (S5), "signed/verified_at_sha" (S4), and "unsigned local" (S3/S4) clauses are carried by named siblings. No uncovered obligation introduced.

Named-consumer / delegation cross-checks (covered-by-named-consumer, not absences):
- This child's What explicitly delegates downstream slices to named siblings — cache/janitor to S2, gate wiring to S3, signing to S4, surfaces to S5 (its Scope "Out:" list). Each delegated obligation is claimed by the named sibling (S2 70c7; S3 fa49; S4 30b3; S5 b7fa). Covered-by-named-consumer.
- The descriptive credential MESSAGE detail this child raises (its AC5) is documented by S6 (1ba5 AC2, which this child cross-refs as "see S6"); the bidirectional hand-off resolves. The portability KNOB docs (REBAR_GATE_TMPDIR, EFS/NFS flock caveat) for the seam this child implements are S6 AC5. Both claimed.
- Fetch prerequisites this child documents inline (uploadpack.allowReachableSHA1InWant, --filter=blob:none preference) are implementation notes within its own scope, not a delegated parent obligation; no unclaimed hand-off.

Note (scope, NOT a G3 finding): the standing G4 dependency-gap on sibling S4 (30b3) — routing the pinned-SHA snapshot handle into plan_review/attest.py's dependency_hashes()/_rehash()/claim-gate freshness re-check across the S3<->S4 seam — is a CONSISTENCY (G4) concern about WHO threads the handle, not a G3 union-coverage gap, and is unrelated to this materialization-core child (this child resolves+pins+materializes the SHA; it does not own the attest.py hashing path). The corresponding parent obligations (AC1 snapshot-only reads, AC2 reproducibility, ref-coherence) remain CLAIMED by children, so the union covers them. G3 union-coverage of the parent criteria is complete.

Conclusion: this child cleanly covers the materialization-core slice (faithful read-tree+checkout-index extraction byte-matching the committed tree incl. export-ignore; LFS/submodule handling; atomic dir-rename population; no-worktree / no index.lock contention; portable tar-free/gettempdir/flock+mkdir-fallback seam; coalesced fetch; descriptive fetch/auth errors) of the parent criteria — it is the PRIMARY OWNER of the faithful-materialization half of parent AC1, the atomic-population/no-worktree half of AC4, the raise-side of AC6, and the implementation of AC7. No parent acceptance/success criterion is left uncovered by the roster. No G3 finding for this pairing.
